reasoning: The baseline for riots in the Republic of Congo (Congo-Brazzaville) is extremely low, averaging only about 3 per year according to the 1.25 reference value. While tensions exist with neighboring DRC, they do not traditionally manifest as mass domestic civilian riots in RoC, which remains under the firm, authoritarian control of President Denis Sassou Nguesso. For the nearest resolution date (June 15), the 30-day window is already nearly complete with no evidence of riots, making it highly improbable. Probabilities for later dates are marginally higher solely due to the increased uncertainty inherent in longer time horizons (e.g. abrupt change in leadership), but remain extremely low overall.
